title: Cranberry and orange scones
subtitle: Fruit scones with dried cranberries and orange zest
excerpt: Cranberry and orange scones have dried cranberries and orange zest worked through a standard scone dough, with a brushed orange-juice glaze added after baking. The cranberries provide tartness against the sweet scone and the orange zest is strong enough to perfume each bite. Best within an hour of baking.
sourceNotes: Cranberry and orange is a flavour combination widely documented in North American and British domestic baking from the late twentieth century. The scone form here follows the standard British scone formula, adapted with dried cranberries and citrus zest.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freeze baked; reheat from frozen in a low oven for 12 minutes.

content.text: Cranberry and orange scones are a fruit scone with dried cranberries providing sharp, jammy pockets and orange zest (generous orange zest, at least two oranges) throughout the dough. An orange-juice glaze brushed on the warm scone after baking adds a slight shine and intensifies the citrus. The scone itself follows the same formula as any fruit scone: cold butter, brief mixing, no twisting the cutter.

items.prepNote: juice only, for the post-bake glaze

content.text: Sift the flour and baking powder into a large bowl. Rub in the cold butter until the mixture is like coarse breadcrumbs. Stir in the sugar, orange zest, and cranberries. Add most of the buttermilk and bring together with a palette knife in 8 to 10 strokes. The dough should be soft and just cohesive. Turn out onto a floured surface, pat to 3 cm thick, and cut rounds with a 5 cm cutter; press straight down, no twist. Place on a lined baking tray.

Heat the oven to 220°C fan. Brush the tops with beaten egg. Bake for 13 to 15 minutes until risen and golden. Mix the orange juice and icing sugar together into a thin glaze. As soon as the scones come out of the oven, brush the glaze over the tops while still hot. Cool briefly on a wire rack and eat warm.
